prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap of molecular oxygen (O2)
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`  (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI-J density-fitted) in PySCF  (chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz; treated as a fixed geometry (single-point evaluation, no re-optimization) → notation DFT//input-xyz.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge 0, multiplicity 3 (open-shell UKS treatment).
SCF convergence: achieved in 7 cycles; `scf_converged: true`.
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine.
Live log: during execution the PySCF stdout/stderr streamed to a time-stamped frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; path was announced at run start and is available for inspection.

Results (all orbital energies in eV, Koopmans convention):
 • HOMO (index 15): –8.8739616 eV
 • LUMO (index 16): –3.5859031 eV
 • HOMO–LUMO gap: 5.2880586 eV
Additional Koopmans-type descriptors: vertical IP 8.8739616 eV; vertical EA 3.5859031 eV; electronegativity 6.2299323 eV; chemical hardness 2.6440293 eV; electrophilicity index 7.3395664 eV.

All quantities were computed via chemkit’s `frontier-orbitals` skill invoking PySCF DFT at B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI-J; provenance: computed.
